channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: China seeks Southeast Asia gains as US influence falters
item.description: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi visited Southeast Asia to present Beijing as a steady partner amid US reliability doubts. Foreign Minister Wang Yi's visit to Southeast Asia shows how Beijing is trying to present itself as a steady regional partner as Southeast Asia grapples with war, tariffs and doubts over Washington's reliability.

item.title: Mali’s Defense Minister Killed in Attacks by Islamist Insurgents
item.description: Mali's Defense Minister Gen Sadio Camara was killed in attacks by Islamist insurgents amid escalating regional violence. The death of the minister, Gen. Sadio Camara, a central figure in the country’s military government, comes amid escalating violence in the region.

item.title: How does targeting water supply during war worsen the scarcity crisis?
item.description: Water infrastructure is being targeted in wars, worsening scarcity crises. We explore why water infrastructure is increasingly being targeted in the midst of war and conflict. How does targeting water supply during war worsen the scarcity crisis?

item.title: Why anti-US sentiment is quietly growing in India
item.description: Indians face economic pain from the US-Israel war with Iran, turning public sentiment against President Donald Trump. Indians are facing economic pain from the US-Israel war with Iran, and public sentiment toward President Donald Trump is turning sour.

item.title: How Trump Event Shooting Became Conspiracy Magnet
item.description: A Trump event shooting sparked conspiracy theories with "staged" appearing in over 300,000 X posts by Sunday. The word "staged" appeared in more than 300,000 posts on X by midday Sunday.

item.title: Have US-Iran talks failed? Why no deal yet doesn’t mean diplomacy is dead
item.description: US-Iran diplomacy is at a deadlock with no deal in sight. Analysts believe both sides are unlikely to return to fighting, suggesting diplomacy remains a viable option despite current challenges.

item.title: Lebanon cannot be bombed into sovereignty
item.description: Lebanon's sovereignty and Hezbollah's demilitarization require a credible political transition. Analysts argue that bombing is not a solution, and a political approach is needed to achieve stability. A sovereign Lebanon and a demilitarised Hezbollah cannot be achieved without a credible political transition.

item.title: Hezbollah Says It Will Keep Weapons as Lebanon Says Israeli Strikes Killed 14
item.description: Hezbollah leader Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah stated that they will keep their weapons. This comes as Israel and Hezbollah have been trading daily attacks despite a cease-fire, with 14 reported killed in recent Israeli strikes.

item.title: Hezbollah leader rejects Lebanon-Israel direct talks, vows to confront Israel
item.description: Hezbollah leader rejected direct Lebanon-Israel talks, vowing to confront Israel. This stance comes after Lebanon and Israel's US ambassadors held meetings in Washington, the first of their kind in decades.

item.title: Iran offers to reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts its blockade and the war ends: Officials
item.description: Iran offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if the US lifts its blockade and the war ends. This proposal would delay negotiations on Iran's nuclear program, according to officials.

item.title: Mali in turmoil after insurgents seize towns and kill defence minister
item.description: Mali's defence minister and military intelligence chief were killed in a weekend assault by jihadists and separatist rebels, who seized several towns and military bases. The attacks, coordinated by al-Qaida-affiliated groups, have left Mali reeling.

item.title: Iran's flurry of diplomacy, as Trump insists U.S. has 'the cards'
item.description: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Russia, seeking to gain leverage and foreign backing as peace talks with the US remain on hold. The visit comes after a whirlwind weekend of diplomacy, with Iran's foreign minister meeting with several countries.

item.title: G7 declaration recognizes land degradation, drought as global security risks
item.description: The G7 has recognized land degradation and drought as global security risks. The declaration highlights the growing threat posed by environmental factors to international stability and security. G7 declaration recognizes land degradation, drought as global security risks EnviroNews Nigeria.

item.title: Bomb blast on Colombia highway leaves at least 20 dead ahead of May elections
item.description: A bomb blast on Colombia's Pan-American Highway killed at least 20 people and injured 36, amid a surge of violence ahead of presidential elections. The attack, blamed on a dissident Farc faction, left buses and vans mangled.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions
item.description: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif that the US should remove operational obstacles, including the blockade, to revive talks. The talks collapsed with Iran blaming the US for the failure. Pezeshkian's minister arrived for talks.

item.title: Somalia hunger crisis worsens as drought displaces more than 500,000 people
item.description: Somalia's drought has displaced over 500,000 people, exacerbating a hunger crisis in the country. Displaced communities face starvation as humanitarian funds decrease, leaving them without assistance or hope. Somalia hunger crisis worsens as drought displaces more than 500,000 people.

item.title: World Cup 2026 prize money, fees to be increased for all teams: FIFA
item.description: FIFA will increase prize money and fees for all teams participating in the 2026 World Cup to help cover participation costs. The move aims to support teams financially. Football's global governing body promises to increase funding for the tournament to help cover participation costs.

item.title: Worker killed at Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant after drone strike, says Russia
item.description: A worker was killed at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power station after a Ukrainian drone attack, according to Russian forces. The plant is under Russian control. A worker at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power ​station has been killed in a ​Ukrainian drone attack, according to Russian ​forces, which control the plant.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In
item.description: Iran's minister arrived in Russia as ceasefire talks between the US, Israel, and Iran collapsed. Iran blamed the US for the collapse of the talks. US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Blames US For Collapse Of Talks As Minister Arrives In Russia NDTV.

item.title: XAU/USD hesitates around $4,700 with geopolitics, central banks eyed
item.description: Gold prices hesitated around $4,700 due to geopolitics and central bank actions. Market participants are closely watching these factors for future price movements. Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD hesitates around $4,700 with geopolitics, central banks eyed FXStreet.
